perturbation.original_text: 3. Ownership and Return. All Confidential Information is and will remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and no right or license is granted to Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information. Upon the termination by ei ther party of discussions concerning the Purpose of this Agreement, or sooner if so requested by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information, including all copies of the same. Upon request, the fact of any such destruction must be certified in writing to the Disclosing Party by an officer of the Receiving Party. Nothing in this Agreement obligates either party hereto to disclose any information to the other or creates any agency or partnership relation between them.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Ownership and Return. All Confidential Information is and will remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and a limited right and license is granted to Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information for the Purpose of this agreement. Upon the termination by either party of discussions concerning the Purpose of this Agreement, or sooner if so requested by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will retain all Confidential Information, including all copies of the same, for archival purposes. Nothing in this Agreement obligates either party hereto to disclose any information to the other or creates any agency or partnership relation between them.
perturbation.explanation: This change grants the Receiving Party 'a limited right and license' to Confidential Information, which **contradicts** the original statement that 'no right or license is granted'. Furthermore, it now mandates that the Receiving Party 'retain all Confidential Information... for archival purposes', which is in direct contradiction to the original requirement to 'return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Ownership and Return. All Confidential Information is and will remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and a limited right and license is granted to Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information for the Purpose of this agreement. Upon the termination by either party of discussions concerning the Purpose of this Agreement, or sooner if so requested by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will retain all Confidential Information, including all copies of the same, for archival purposes. Nothing in this Agreement obligates either party hereto to disclose any information to the other or creates any agency or partnership relation between them.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3 explicitly grants 'a limited right and license' to the Receiving Party, directly contradicting the original statement in the original text that 'no right or license is granted.' It also changes the requirement to 'return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information' to 'retain all Confidential Information... for archival purposes'. This directly clashes with the contradicted text since it mandates retention instead of return or destruction of the confidential information. The explanation confirms this creates a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Remedies. Each party hereto acknowledges that remedies at law may be inadequate to protect the other party against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to t he parties hereto, agrees to the granting of injunctive relief in favor of the other without proof of irreparable harm or the posting of any bond. In the event of litigation commenced by a party to enforce this Agreement, the primarily prevailing party will be entitled to recover its costs and expenses in such litigation, including attorneys’ fees and expenses.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Remedies. Each party hereto acknowledges that remedies at law are adequate to protect the other party against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to the parties hereto, agrees to waive any rights to injunctive relief. In the event of litigation commenced by a party to enforce this Agreement, each party will bear their own costs and expenses in such litigation, including attorneys’ fees and expenses.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a **direct contradiction** by stating that 'remedies at law are adequate', which is the opposite of the original statement that remedies at law 'may be inadequate'. It further negates the possibility of injunctive relief and removes the provision for the prevailing party to recover costs, making the enforcement of the agreement less certain and potentially ineffective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Remedies. Each party hereto acknowledges that remedies at law are adequate to protect the other party against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to the parties hereto, agrees to waive any rights to injunctive relief. In the event of litigation commenced by a party to enforce this Agreement, each party will bear their own costs and expenses in such litigation, including attorneys’ fees and expenses.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. It first states that remedies at law are adequate, and then states that injunctive relief is waived, which implies remedies at law are not adequate, as injunctive relief is a remedy. The contradiction occurs within the same section of the contract, making it a clear and direct in-text contradiction, as also highlighted by the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Transaction. Both parties agree that unless and until final, written definitive agreements regarding a specific transaction between the parties have been executed and delivered, neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever to extend or expand the relationship beyond the Purpose of this Agreement, except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Transaction. Both parties agree that even without final, written definitive agreements regarding a specific transaction between the parties being executed and delivered, either party will be under legal obligation of any kind whatsoever to extend or expand the relationship beyond the Purpose of this Agreement, regardless of the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly **contradicts** the original intent of Section 8. Originally, it stated no obligation exists without a final agreement. Now, it states an obligation exists even without a final agreement, effectively binding the parties beyond the intended 'Purpose' even without a formal agreement. This contradicts the entire principle of the NDA which is for the purpose of considering a potential business relationship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Transaction. Both parties agree that even without final, written definitive agreements regarding a specific transaction between the parties being executed and delivered, either party will be under legal obligation of any kind whatsoever to extend or expand the relationship beyond the Purpose of this Agreement, regardless of the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 8 directly contradicts the original intent by stating that an obligation to extend the relationship exists even without a final agreement. The contradicted text is the changed text in Section 8, which creates a contradiction because the original Section 8 stated no obligation exists without a final agreement. The perturbation explanation confirms this contradiction.
